description_long: This lamp features a central circular mounting base from which six elegantly curved arms extend outward, each supporting a downward-facing, fluted lampshade. The shades are symmetrically arranged in a radial pattern, giving the fixture a balanced and ornate appearance. Each lampshade has a ribbed, scalloped texture, adding a decorative touch to the overall design. The connecting arms are slender and gently arched, converging at the central hub, which is robust and slightly elevated. The entire structure appears to be made of a uniform, light-colored material, likely painted or coated metal with glass or acrylic shades. This style is reminiscent of classic chandeliers often found in upscale urban interiors or public spaces.
description: A six-armed chandelier with ribbed, downward-facing shades and a central circular base, featuring ornate, symmetrical design elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing all six fluted lampshades evenly spaced around the central base, with the arms and shades clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the curvature of the arms and the depth of the shades, with the central hub partially visible.
description_view_2: Another angled view similar to view 1, emphasizing the radial symmetry and the detailed ribbing on the shades.
description_view_3: Rear-angled view, showing the back of the shades and the convergence of the arms at the central mounting base.
